description: Cross-session progress tracker for Claude Code agent work on openaiops-platform. Pairs with features.json (status ledger) and lessons-learned (sediment).
current_focus: SLICE-3 complete (15 tasks, 10/10 ACs verified). Services + topology vertical: cmd/topo-engine 4th binary on :8084 → ReplacingMergeTree topology_edges_v1 + service_stats_v1 with FINAL idempotency → /api/v1/services{,/{name}} + /api/v1/topology on cmd/query → 4 new frontend pages (/overview, /services/:name 6-tab, /topology, /traces/:id Service Map subtab body via client-side derive). Shared <ServiceGraph> (d3-force + hand-rolled SVG) reused 3 places. Zero nginx changes (D4 stays closed; SLICE-2's promise upheld). 26 Playwright + 89 vitest + full backend integration all PASS. Next: SLICE-4 brainstorming (metrics + alerts) — OR first dig into D6 (AdminConn CH-user Row Policy bypass deployment concern that prevents topo-engine from discovering tenants in dev/prod).

recently_completed.name: Slice 0 (Foundation) — full delivery
recently_completed.summary: Bootstrap → Go backend (1.25, chi v5, pgx v5, goose v3) → PG schema (tenants/api_keys/metering_events) → bcrypt + tenant domain types → ApiKey pgx repo (dockertest integration tests) → auth Bearer middleware (ctx.tenant_id injection) → PG resolver + chi gateway server + /healthz → docker-compose stack (PG+Redis+CH+OTel-Collector+Caddy+gateway+frontend, 8080/4317/4318 env-overridable for SignOz collision) → Vue 3 + Vite + Pinia + Naive UI scaffold → openapm design tokens → vue-i18n (zh-CN/en-US) + auth store (TDD) + Vue Router with guard + LoginView + HomeView + AppLayout + TopBar + SideBar → frontend Dockerfile + nginx (with critical /api proxy) + Caddy reverse-proxy → Playwright E2E (4 specs incl. two-tenant cross-context) + GitHub Actions CI (3 jobs: backend / frontend-unit / e2e). CI green 6m32s on run 26359958968. 8/8 ACs verified (AC-7 partial). 14 implementer + 11 reviewer + 2 fixer subagent dispatches over ~3h.

recently_completed.name: SLICE-1 — Trace ingest end-to-end
recently_completed.summary: telemetrygen / hot-r.o.d. → cmd/ingester (OTel otlpreceiver embed, Bearer-on-receiver auth, drops SDK tenant.id) → CH traces_v1 with Row Policy → cmd/query :8081 list+detail → Vue /traces with hand-rolled SVG waterfall. 16 tasks, ~27 commits (incl. review-driven fixes). 8/10 ACs verified by automated tests; AC #2 + AC #6 verified by migrate-ch-up stdout + system.row_policies; AC #7 verified by Playwright. Three real bugs caught: ingester IncludeMetadata missing on both gRPC and HTTP transports; CH migration replay-safety required IF NOT EXISTS on both DDLs; frontend nginx only proxied /api/* to gateway (caught by T16 Playwright when SPA-side /api/v1/traces returned 404 — fixed by mirroring Caddy's split into nginx.conf). New shared chtest package landed under internal/chquery/chtest for future integration tests.

recently_completed.name: SLICE-2 — Log ingest end-to-end
recently_completed.summary: SDK / telemetrygen-logs → cmd/log-ingester (OTel otlpreceiver embed, gRPC :4327 + HTTP :4328, Bearer-on-receiver auth, IncludeMetadata applied on both transports day-1 per SLICE-1 lesson) → CH logs_v1 with Row Policy → GET /api/v1/logs on cmd/query :8081 with full filter suite → Vue /logs page (SeverityBadge + LogRow expand + filter bar + URL source-of-truth) + /traces/:id Logs subtab (LogsPanel, span-click scoping). 14 tasks. Drift D4 closed: nginx /api blocks deleted; Caddy is sole ingress; Playwright test #4 locks the architectural assertion. internal/ingestshared/ extracted for both ingesters to reuse. Five bugs caught: circular import during shared extraction; bodyAsString Map-type needed JSON not Go-syntax; body_contains used LIKE not positionUTF8; LogsPanel double-fetch via suppressNextWatch; cross_tenant sub2/3 passed vacuously due to ingest-inside-t.Run. All 10 ACs verified: AC #2/#6 via CH schema + system.row_policies; AC #1/#3/#5/#8 via Go integration tests (22 sub-tests); AC #4 via handler/repo unit tests + curl; AC #7 via Playwright 5/5 + vitest 52/52; AC #9 via metering integration test; AC #10 via nginx.conf deletion + D4 regression Playwright assertion.

recently_completed.name: SLICE-3 — Services + topology end-to-end
recently_completed.summary: cmd/topo-engine 4th binary on :8084 (1min ticker + cold-start Catchup capped at TOPO_CATCHUP_MAX=1h + per-tenant errgroup limit TOPO_TENANT_CONCURRENCY=4 + 4 Prometheus metrics) → two-Pass RunOnce writes ReplacingMergeTree topology_edges_v1 (per pair) + service_stats_v1 (per service x span_kind), idempotent via FINAL in reads → chquery.AdminConn introduced with lint-confined whitelist (AdminListTenants + AdminMaxBucket only, only callable from internal/topoengine/) → 3 new query handlers (/v1/services, /v1/services/{name}, /v1/topology) all behind Caddy as one-line handle adds (zero nginx changes proves SLICE-2's promise) → 4 new Vue pages (/overview, /services/:name 6-tab NTabs shell, /topology, /traces/:id Service Map subtab body via client-side derive — zero new endpoint) → shared <ServiceGraph> (d3-force + hand-rolled SVG) reused 3 places → <TimeWindowPicker> with ?window= URL source-of-truth + suppressNextWatch composable lifted from SLICE-2 LogsPanel. 15 tasks. 9 bugs caught during execution: ORDER BY missing caller_kind; AdminConn godoc lied; tenants.go silent UUID skip; plan had wrong-format UUIDs; lint rule too narrow for cmd/topo-engine; d3-force needs source/target fields; vue-router test harness needs await router.isReady; useForceSimulation TS2344; AND T15 caught a T11 regression where services.ts + topology.ts used raw fetch() instead of the shared axios client (skipping the Bearer interceptor — 401 on every new page). Regression locked by api/__tests__/client.spec.ts (asserts interceptor injects Bearer from localStorage). 26/26 Playwright PASS (+13 new SLICE-3 specs); 21 vitest files / 89 tests PASS (+10 from T15 api auth regression specs); backend unit + integration (incl. 5 topoengine integration tests + cross-tenant 6 sub-assertions) all PASS. One new known drift D6: topo-engine AdminConn → activeTenants returns 0 in prod because tenant_isolation_traces_v1 Row Policy filters tenant discovery — needs CH user exemption (deployment concern; integration tests already use exempt test CH user).

notes.note: Slice 0 caught 3 real bugs (nginx /api proxy, NaiveUI NInput wrapper, Node 25 Storage.clear missing) + 2 env quirks (SignOz port collision, Go 1.25 drift). See docs/lessons-learned-2026-05-24.md.

Old repo huangbaixun/OpenAIOps archived 2026-05-24 (PR #8 merged, then gh repo archive). All non-doc code is in this new repo. Spec + ADR-0007 also live in this repo as docs/decisions/0001-initial-architecture.md (imported from old repo's docs/specs/2026-05-24-platform-rewrite-design.md).

PRE-3 Task 4 surfaced a clickhouse-go/v2 protocol quirk: per-query Settings sent over the native protocol default to is_important=true, which CH validates against builtin-settings only. Custom names (custom_*) MUST be wrapped in clickhouse.CustomSetting{Value: ...} to flip the flag to 0x02. Also: CH 23.12 server requires custom_settings_prefixes config to allow the custom_ prefix at all. Without BOTH pieces, layer 2 of ADR-0001 §3.3 silently fails — code looks correct, but Row Policy never sees the setting.

SLICE-1 T16 caught a Slice-0 regression: frontend nginx.conf only forwards /api/* → gateway. Once a second query backend appeared at :8081 (cmd/query), the SPA on the frontend host port could not reach it — /api/v1/traces returned nginx 404. Fix duplicated the rule in two places, which is fragile. SLICE-2 T8 resolved this properly: nginx /api blocks deleted entirely; Caddy is now the sole ingress. New query-side routes only need a one-line Caddy handle — zero nginx changes. Drift D4 closed.

SLICE-2 T14: Playwright E2E specs must use relative paths (page.goto('/foo')) and rely on playwright.config.ts baseURL = https://localhost (Caddy ingress). Never hardcode a port-based BASE constant — local machines may have that port intercepted by OrbStack or other tools. traces.spec.ts was updated in T14 to remove the hardcoded BASE = http://localhost:3000. All future E2E specs follow the logs.spec.ts pattern.

SLICE-2 cross_tenant sub2/sub3 passed vacuously before T11 review fix: the ingest call was inside t.Run for sub1, so if sub1 ran correctly but sub2/3 got no data, they trivially passed. Always hoist shared setup (ingest calls, schema seeding) outside t.Run blocks to ensure later sub-tests actually exercise the deny-path with real data.

SLICE-3 T15 caught a SLICE-3 T11 regression that survived 4 review passes + 3 Playwright runs of OTHER specs: services.ts + topology.ts used raw fetch('/api/v1/...') instead of routing through the shared api/client.ts axios instance. The shared client's request interceptor (auto-injects Bearer from localStorage.apiKey) was therefore skipped, and every /overview / /topology / /services/:name page hit 401. The vitest specs originally written for T11 mocked global fetch() and asserted the URL string — they never noticed the missing Authorization header. Lesson: ANY new API module MUST import the shared client and test specs should assert client.get(path, ...) was called, not the raw URL string. Regression locked by api/__tests__/client.spec.ts which directly invokes the request interceptor and asserts the Bearer header gets stamped. Apply this checklist to every future api/ module added (SLICE-4 metrics + alerts will land 4-6 of these).

SLICE-3 T15 also caught a multi-container staleness pattern: when query+topo-engine handlers land across T8/T9 commits and frontend pages land across T12-T14 but the running compose stack is days old, `make seed-topology` succeeds (writes traces) but every downstream verification appears broken because the running containers don't have the new code. Cure: `docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml build query topo-engine frontend && up -d` BEFORE running any e2e verification in the closing T15 evidence loop. Add this to future closing-task playbooks.

SLICE-3 first slice that proves SLICE-2's nginx-deletion was sound: added 2 new query routes (/api/v1/services*, /api/v1/topology*) with ZERO frontend/nginx.conf changes. Each new route was a single 4-line Caddy handle. Future slices (metrics, alerts) repeat this pattern. The architectural assertion 'one-line Caddy adds for future query routes' is now proven, not promised.

known_drift.item: AC-7 (goose down rollback) has SQL but no automated CI test. Slice 1 should add a CI step that runs goose down after goose up and re-asserts the schema diff.

PGResolver does full table scan + bcrypt-verify all active keys per request. Acceptable at N<100 keys. Slice 1's ingester traffic will expose this. Add key_prefix CHAR(8) column for candidate narrowing.
migrate service in docker-compose installs goose via go install at every compose-up. Network-dependent + slow cold start. Switch to ghcr.io/pressly/goose image or pre-bake into Dockerfile.

List ts upper-bound uses `time.Now().UTC()` with +1s lookahead (drift D1 mitigation added in SLICE-2 T7). Still an edge-case: if the query backend clock and ingester clock drift >1s, very recent records may still miss the window. Full fix is `ts <=` semantics or a configurable lookahead.
known_drift.tracked_in: SLICE-1 T13 implementer note + SLICE-1-evidence.txt D1 + SLICE-2 T7 mitigation

known_drift.item: cmd/seed-traces honors INGESTER_OTLP_GRPC_HOST_PORT but doesn't auto-discover. Manual env var needed on machines with port collisions (e.g., this dev machine uses 14317 due to .env override).

AdminConn cross-tenant visibility in production requires the CH user (used by topo-engine) to be exempted from tenant_isolation_* Row Policies. Currently undocumented in IaC; CLAUDE.md doesn't mention this prod requirement. SLICE-3 T8 deploy task should encode this (DDL adding the bypass policy or a comment on the CH user's GRANT block).
known_drift.tracked_in: SLICE-3 T2 review (commit 442be9f) + admin.go AdminConn godoc

known_drift.item: RESOLVED 2026-05-27: frontend/src/components/ServiceGraph/useForceSimulation.ts TS2344 (vue-tsc) cleared by making GraphNode extend SimulationNodeDatum and introducing a SimEdge (extends SimulationLinkDatum<GraphNode>) for the d3-force generic, while GraphEdge keeps caller/callee + optional source/target for API callers. useForceSimulation maps GraphEdge -> SimEdge before forceLink. `npm run build` now clean; vitest 76/76 PASS.
known_drift.tracked_in: SLICE-3 T13 implementer note + T13 review fix commit

open_questions.q: Phase 2 sibling repo openaiops-ai — when to start? ADR-0007 said 'immediately after MVP'. But MVP = Slice 5 done, which is ~40 weeks of solo work away. Practical answer is probably 'after Slice 2 or 3' so AI agents have trace+log data to work with. Revisit at end of Slice 2.

resolved_questions.decision: Archived via gh repo archive on 2026-05-24. Read-only. PR #8 merged the pivot ADR-0007 + spec + plan into old repo's main before archive.

resolved_questions.q: PRE-1: ClickHouse schema init mechanism — initdb.d vs ch-migrate service?
resolved_questions.decision: Chose ch-migrate service symmetric to PG goose (Option B). Scaffolded: ADR-0002 + deploy/ch-migrate.sh + deploy/docker-compose.yml ch-migrate entry + backend/ch-migrations/ + Makefile migrate-ch-up target + CLAUDE.md 'CH 迁移' rule block. Forward-only, plain SQL files (no goose pragmas), idempotent via _schema_migrations tracking table. Smoke-verified all 3 paths: empty-dir no-op, apply, skip-already-applied. ALTER TABLE arrival triggers ADR-0002 revisit.

resolved_questions.q: PRE-2: Query API deployment shape — extend gateway vs separate cmd/query/?
resolved_questions.decision: Chose Option B (split cmd/query/ from day 1) per ADR-0003. Brainstormed via /harness:brainstorming. Decision driven by 2 confirmed assumptions: (1) workload Heavy (server-side aggregation known coming), (2) Heavy arrives SLICE-1/2 (within ~1 month). Under Heavy + soon, original progress.json Option-A recommendation inverts because future-split cost gets paid by the first query that saturates gateway CPU. Spec at docs/specs/2026-05-24-pre-2-query-api-deployment-design.md; ADR at docs/decisions/0003-query-api-deployment-shape.md. CLAUDE.md '二进制 + 路由划分' block added. Locks: chquery.MustTenantScope signature; metering write-path-only; multi-binary single-image Dockerfile pattern.

resolved_questions.q: PRE-3: how to implement MustTenantScope + Row Policy + lint?
resolved_questions.decision: Three-layer defense landed per plan docs/plans/2026-05-24-pre-3-must-tenant-scope-plan.md. Layer 1: chquery.MustTenantScope helper validates 'tenant_id = ?' (SELECT) or '(tenant_id,' (INSERT) shape + panics on missing tenant. Layer 2: chquery.Conn wraps clickhouse-go/v2 driver and injects custom_tenant_id session setting via clickhouse.CustomSetting{} wrapper (discovered during smoke testing — raw values get rejected as builtin-setting). CH server must have custom_settings_prefixes=custom_ config (deploy/clickhouse-custom-settings.xml bind-mounted in compose). CH Row Policy template provided in backend/ch-migrations/README.md for SLICE-1 migrations. Layer 3: cross-tenant reverse E2E is SLICE-1 AC #8 (needs traces_v1 table first). Build-time lint deploy/lint-no-bare-ch.sh + make lint-ch + CI step forbids bare clickhouse-go imports outside internal/chquery. 7 commits 80344d7..ce7ed1b. Dockertest smoke (TestSmoke_TenantIsolation) validates layer 1+2 against real CH 23.12.

Resolved by extending chquery.Conn.PrepareBatch with a *chquery.Batch wrapper (commit 1a00986). Tenant_id pinned at PrepareBatch time and verified on each Append; CH Row Policy is layer-2 backstop. Smoke test TestSmoke_BatchTenantIsolation proves tenant-A-writes / tenant-B-reads = 0.

resolved_questions.q: SLICE-2 D4: should nginx be kept with dual-write routing or eliminated?
resolved_questions.decision: Eliminated nginx /api routing entirely (SLICE-2 T8). nginx.conf now has only the SPA fallback (try_files). Caddy at :443 is the sole API ingress. Future query routes (services, topology) only need one-line Caddy handles — zero nginx changes. Regression locked by Playwright test #4 in logs.spec.ts.
